task: Deep carve of three deferred monoliths via dataclass / phase-grouping / pure-helper extraction; behavioural-equivalence required for all touched paths.

_ensembling_score.py.behavioural_eq_evidence: 89 ensembling tests across tests/test_ensembling_quality_gate.py + tests/training/test_ensembling_ensembling*.py + tests/training/test_ensembling_composition_metadata.py + tests/training/test_regression_w9e_ensembling_fixes.py + tests/training/test_ensemble_val_only_for_es.py + tests/training/test_member_quality_gate_nanmedian.py + tests/training/test_combine_probs_median_nanmedian.py + tests/models/test_monolith_split_w11a_ensembling_score.py + tests/models/test_monolith_split_w12a_ensembling_score.py + tests/models/test_monolith_split_w14a_ensembling_score.py all GREEN post-carve.
_ensembling_score.py.dataclass_rationale: Pure-helper extraction proved sufficient -- the seven sub-bodies have well-defined inputs/outputs and only mutate listed res-dict keys. A state dataclass would have added boilerplate without LOC saving.
_ensembling_score.py.notes: compute_member_quality_gate is injected via a kwarg (default None -> internal import) so existing tests that monkey-patch the parent's module-level binding continue to hit the spy. Parent retains the module-level import (with # noqa: F401 -- monkey-patched by tests) so test_ensemble_val_only_for_es.py::test_member_quality_gate_routes_to_oof_not_val passes.

_main_train_suite.py.dataclass_rationale: ctx (TrainingContext) already carries every cross-phase mutable; a MainSuiteState dataclass would have duplicated ctx with no semantic gain. Phase-grouping into pure helpers achieved the <700 budget without it.
_main_train_suite.py.notes: apply_module_global_patches takes ``module`` so the helper can rebind the module-global apply_loky_cpu_count_override / apply_third_party_patches_once on first call -- preserves test monkeypatch surface. Removed dead-locals readback after the post-loop helper extraction (the original ctx -> local mirror of train_df_polars / pipeline / defer_pandas_conv / baseline_rss_mb / *_size_bytes_cached / trainset_features_stats / slug_to_original_target_{type,name} was already unread by the surrounding code; deleting it cost zero behaviour).

_phase_train_one_target_body.py.target_met_reason: Hit 1069 LOC vs <700 target. The body grew from 933 to 1190 between the task brief and the start of this wave because parallel agent W14B added ~250 LOC of MLP extreme-AR helpers (_apply_mlp_extreme_ar_output_activation, _apply_mlp_extreme_ar_weight_decay_bump, _identify_per_well_columns, _drop_columns_for_mlp, _get_per_well_pattern) at module scope on top of the existing monolith. Three pure helpers were carved safely (-121 LOC); the remaining ~880-LOC function body is the deeply-nested pre_pipeline / mlframe_model / weight_schema loop with 25+ closure-captured locals (orig_pre_pipeline, ens_models, weight_schemas, strategy_by_model, sorted_models, _suite_feature_cache, _per_pp_cache, tier_dfs_cache, tier_enum_map_cache, prepared_frames_cache, prev_tier, _non_neural_train_times, _total_models_in_run, _model_idx_in_run, _break_model_loop, _mlp_extreme_ar_fired / _mlp_ea_lag1, _ngb_fallback_snapshot, _cb_extra_fit_invariant, polars_fastpath_active, prepared_train/val/test, _xgb_category_map, _cat_features, tier_pandas, train_df_transformed / val_df_transformed / test_df_transformed). A safe extraction requires the OneTargetBodyState dataclass refactor planned in the task brief, paired with a comprehensive fuzz fixture (synthetic deterministic train/val/test multi-target run with sample_weights + multiple pre_pipelines + multiple strategies) as the byte-identical behavioural-equivalence witness. Per the original task brief: 'Если behavioural-equivalence НЕ achievable... DEFER carve, manifest reason.' Deferred. Recommendation for next wave: dedicated PR with biz_val fuzz fixture pinned BEFORE any phase extraction; the OneTargetBodyState dataclass should capture pre_pipeline / mlframe_model / weight loop state separately so per-loop extractions can land without dragging the surrounding orchestration.
_phase_train_one_target_body.py.dataclass_rationale: OneTargetBodyState was planned but not built; the deepest body remains intact pending the dedicated fuzz-fixture-backed PR.

deviations_from_plan.deviation: Hit 1069 LOC vs <700 target (-121 vs the -490 implied by 1190 -> 700).
deviations_from_plan.rationale: Single-function monolith with no inner function defs and 25+ closure-captured locals shared across the pre_pipeline / mlframe_model / weight_schema nested loops. A behavioural carve requires the OneTargetBodyState dataclass refactor planned in the task brief, plus a fuzz fixture as the byte-identical equivalence witness. Per the original wave-12B / 12A deferral rationale + the task brief's risk-mitigation clause: deferred. Three safe pure-helper extractions landed (-121 LOC).

Reject any extraction that introduces numerical drift.
